_metadata.description: Global cross-organ propagation rules. Combine two strategies: (1) hand-written rules for medically-specific propagation, (2) atlas-derived defaults — what each atlas connection_type transmits and with what decay.

atlas_connection_semantics._comment: How NEXUS interprets each atlas connection_type for state propagation. decay = fraction of state passed along; polarity tells whether the receiving state goes up or down when source state crosses threshold.

arterial.rationale: if source organ's perfusion drops, downstream (arterial) targets also lose perfusion

venous.rationale: venous congestion transmits backward to upstream organs

lymphatic.rationale: lymphatic drainage carries infection / inflammation downstream

neural.rationale: nerve activation propagates with high fidelity

referred_pain.rationale: visceral pain referred to dermatome via convergence at spinal level

adjacent.rationale: severe local inflammation can extend to anatomically adjacent organ

circulates_through.rationale: blood circulation distributes infections, toxins, cytokines systemically

gi_tract.rationale: GI tract continuity allows contents/pathogens to move downstream

urinary.rationale: obstruction propagates retrograde in urinary tract

biliary.rationale: obstruction propagates retrograde in biliary tree

airway.rationale: airway lumen connects bronchi to alveoli; obstruction or infection moves through

portal.rationale: portal hypertension affects all portal drainage organs

cardiac.rationale: cardiac function directly affects connected vasculature

part_of._comment: part_of is anatomical containment, not a propagation channel — no automatic state transfer

global_propagation_rules.name: shock_renal
global_propagation_rules.rationale: Hypotensive shock from low cardiac output → renal hypoperfusion → pre-renal AKI

global_propagation_rules.name: left_heart_failure_pulmonary
global_propagation_rules.rationale: Left ventricular failure → pulmonary venous congestion → alveolar edema → impaired O2 diffusion

global_propagation_rules.name: pyelonephritis_to_blood
global_propagation_rules.rationale: Pyelonephritis can seed bloodstream → bacteremia / urosepsis risk

global_propagation_rules.name: cystitis_ascending_to_kidney
global_propagation_rules.rationale: Ascending UTI: bladder pathogens reflux up ureters → kidney → pyelonephritis

global_propagation_rules.name: gi_dehydration_to_kidney
global_propagation_rules.rationale: Severe diarrhea → volume depletion → reduced renal perfusion → pre-renal AKI

neural_framework_placeholder._comment: Reserved for Stage D.4 — neural propagation network. Will encode: dermatome → organ mapping (referred pain), cranial nerve pathways, spinal cord segments, autonomic projections. Currently empty.

vascular_framework_placeholder._comment: Reserved for Stage D.5 — vascular propagation network. Will encode: arterial perfusion territories, venous drainage, watershed regions, embolic sources, anastomoses. Currently empty.
